statement.p: Brandon has {\$85} in his piggy bank. He plans to purchase some Pokemon cards, which costs {\$1.95} each. He plans to save {\$51.85} to purchase another toy. At most how many Pokemon cards can he purchase?
Write an equation to solve this problem.
Brandon can purchase at most Pokemon cards.

solution.p: Assume Brandon can purchase x Pokemon cards.
Each card costs {\$1.95}, so x cards will cost 1.95x dollars.
Currently, Brandon has {\$85} in his piggy bank. After spending 1.95x dollars, he would have 85-1.95x dollars left.
Brandon will keep {\$51.85} unspent. Now we can write an equation:
Brandon can purchase at most 17 Pokemon cards.
